name: azure-security-keyvault-keys-dotnet
description: A skill for working with Azure Key Vault keys using the Azure SDK for .NET. Provides code generation, best practices, and examples for cryptographic key management operations.
capabilities: Generate code for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting keys in Azure Key Vault
Provide examples for key rotation and lifecycle management operations
Show how to use Azure Identity for secure authentication
Demonstrate backup and restore operations for keys
Explain key permissions and access policies
Provide code for encryption and decryption using vault keys
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 potential security issues. Risk score: 0/100. This skill provides guidance and code generation templates for Azure Key Vault key operations using the official Azure SDK for .NET.
valueStatement: Developers struggle with Azure Key Vault key management APIs and secure cryptographic practices. This skill provides ready-to-use .NET code snippets, best practices, and implementation guidance for storing, retrieving, and managing cryptographic keys in Azure Key Vault.
userTitle: Generate Azure Key Vault key code
limitations: Does not execute actual Azure operations - provides guidance only
Requires valid Azure subscription and configured Key Vault
Does not handle network connectivity issues or Azure service errors
User must understand Azure RBAC and Key Vault access policies

useCases.targetUser: .NET developers new to Azure Key Vault
useCases.title: Generate key creation code
useCases.description: Create new cryptographic keys in Azure Key Vault with custom key properties

useCases.title: Implement key rotation
useCases.description: Set up automated key rotation with rotation policies and notifications

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers handling key backup procedures
useCases.title: Backup and restore keys
useCases.description: Export and import keys securely for disaster recovery scenarios

promptTemplates.title: Create a new key
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a new RSA key in Azure Key Vault using the Azure SDK for .NET. Include code for setting key size and expiration.

promptTemplates.title: Retrieve and use a key
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I retrieve an existing key from Azure Key Vault and use it for encryption in my .NET application?

promptTemplates.title: Set up key rotation
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure automatic key rotation for a key in Azure Key Vault. Show how to set rotation policy and lifetime actions.

promptTemplates.title: Backup and restore key
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate code to backup a key to a secure location and restore it when needed. Include error handling.

bestPractices: Use Azure Identity with Managed Identity in production environments to avoid storing credentials
Set appropriate expiration times for keys and implement regular rotation policies
Use separate Key Vaults for different environments (dev, staging, production)
antiPatterns: Storing Key Vault URLs or credential information in source code
Granting excessive permissions to key operations beyond what is needed
Skipping error handling when interacting with Key Vault operations

faq.question: What authentication methods does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill demonstrates Azure Identity with DefaultAzureCredential, Managed Identity, and service principal authentication.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with hardware security modules?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure Key Vault supports HSM-protected keys. The skill includes examples for creating HSM keys.

faq.question: How do I handle key permissions in my application?
faq.answer: Configure Key Vault access policies or use Azure RBAC to grant specific permissions to your application identity.

faq.question: What key types are supported?
faq.answer: Azure Key Vault supports RSA, EC (Elliptic Curve), and AES symmetric keys. The skill covers RSA and EC keys.

faq.question: Can I import existing keys?
faq.answer: Yes, use the ImportKey method to bring existing keys into Azure Key Vault. The skill provides import examples.

faq.question: How does key versioning work?
faq.answer: Each key operation can create a new version. The key client retrieves the latest version by default, or you can specify a version.
